rulings.Case Title: THE CANDLESTICK - THE COVE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. TOP VISION DEVELOPMENT, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder By Defendants M M Lightweight Concrete Company, Nicodemus Plumbing And Milo Masonry To Top Vision Development, Llcs Motion To Set Aside Case Management Order No. 1
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 4, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANTS NICODEMUS PLIMBING & MECHANICAL, MILO MASONRY, INC., M & M LIGHTWEIGHT CONCRETE COMPANY, INC.'S Joinder To Top Vision Development, LLC's Motion To Set Aside Case Management Order No. 1. The court grants this motion in part. Defendant demonstrates good cause to withdraw the stipulation and order regarding the appointment of a special master. Defendant agreed that Mr. Henningsen could serve as special master and he is no longer available. The court has no authority under Jovine v. FHP, Inc. (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1506, 1522, to impose a new special master on the parties. The court vacates CMO1 because it is largely outdated since there is no longer a "special master" to control the litigation. Under Lu v. Sup. Crt. (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 1264, there is good cause to appoint a discovery referee to manage discovery disputes in this action per CCP 639(a)(5). Plaintiff is bringing a complex $11 million construction defect action involving numerous parties and the referee can develop a discovery plan that reduces duplicative and cumbersome discovery. The court enters a discovery stay limited in duration until a referee is appointed to manage the discovery in this case. The court recommends retired Comm. Best if available. The court continues this motion until July 1, 2015, to give the parties a chance to meet and confer regarding the selection of a discovery referee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SCOTT SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Pursuant To C.C.P.§ 2031.300 [Documents] And C.C.P. § 2031.320 [Documents]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday June 4, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Pursuant To C.C.P.§ 2031.300 [Documents] And C.C.P. § 2031.320 [Documents]. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED IN FULL. The defendant's late-filed papers were not considered and the motion was reviewed as unopposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ORVILLE MEAUX VS. PAUL J. STEINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 4, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT PAUL STEINER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is granted. Proving actual damages is an essential element of a malpractice claim. "The mere breach of a professional duty, causing only nominal damages, speculative harm, or the threat of future harm - harm not yet realized - does not suffice to create a cause of action for negligence." (Budd v. Nixen (1971) 6 Cal.3d 195, 200.) Defendant met his initial burden by presenting evidence showing that Plaintiff has no evidence of actual damages. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts 18-22.) Plaintiff does not raise a triable issue of material fact in opposition. Defendant's objections to Exhibits F and G to the Declaration of Orville Meaux are sustained. Exhibit F, a purported copy of a forensic accounting performed at the request of Plaintiff by his expert, is inadmissible because there is no declaration from the expert explaining the expert's qualifications, what he relied on in putting together the spreadsheet, or how the spreadsheet applies to the facts of this case. (See Kelly v. Trunk (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 519, 523-24.) Exhibit G, a declaration from Plaintiff's human resources expert, is inadmissible because it is not executed under penalty of perjury. (CCP sec. 2015.5; Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corporation (2014) 33 Cal.4th 601.) Thus, the only admissible evidence submitted on the issue of actual damages is Plaintiff's own opinion of what he believes the underlying malpractice case was worth. This is insufficient to raise a triable issue of material fact. (See Marshak v. Ballesteros (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 1514, 1519.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. ALEXEI KUCHINSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 4, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT ALEXEI KUCHINSKY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff's common count causes of action are well-pleaded. The only essential allegations are (1) the statement of indebtedness in a certain sum, (2) the consideration, and (3) nonpayment. See Witkin, Cal. Procedure, Pleading § 518. Defendant's citation to Otworth v. So. Pacific Trans. Co. (1985) 166 Cal.App.3d 452, is off point because plaintiff is not suing for breach of contract. Defendant's special demurrers are improper because this is a limited jurisdiction case. See CCP 92(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
